title: Palicourea stenoclada
taxonomy: Palicourea stenoclada belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. This classification places Palicourea stenoclada within the order Gentianales, a group of plants that also includes coffee and gardenias. The genus Palicourea itself is a group of flowering plants native to tropical regions of Central and South America. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Palicourea stenoclada is closely related to other plants in this family, such as Gardenia and Coffea. Further classification includes the species being part of the subfamily Rubioideae.
uses: Palicourea stenoclada has been traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world. The plant is reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential medicinal uses. In some regions, it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. Its leaves and stems are often extracted to prepare remedies, such as teas and infusions. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential and safety of Palicourea stenoclada.

intro: Palicourea stenoclada, also known as "Kola", is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Palicourea stenoclada include alkaloids, such as stenocladine and stenocladine hydrochloride, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Palicourea stenoclada is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, or used in the form of a tincture or extract to harness its health benefits.
